author: geunhwan
title: Docker build fails - Azure Container Registry OAuth token fetch refused
signature: failed to fetch oauth token docker registry connection refused|External Service

failed_jobs.name: Docker
failed_jobs.symptom: docker buildx build failed: cannot fetch oauth token from openvinogithubactions.azurecr.io (connection refused)

key_errors: #2 ERROR: failed to authorize: failed to fetch oauth token: Post "https://openvinogithubactions.azurecr.io/oauth2/token": dial tcp 172.16.0.197:443: connect: connection refused
ERROR: failed to solve: failed to fetch oauth token: Post "https://openvinogithubactions.azurecr.io/oauth2/token": dial tcp 172.16.0.197:443: connect: connection refused
RuntimeError: Command 'docker buildx build ...' failed with exit code 1
root_cause: The Docker build job attempted to pull a base image (ubuntu:22.04) from the internal Azure Container Registry mirror (openvinogithubactions.azurecr.io) but the TCP connection to 172.16.0.197:443 was refused. This is a transient network/infrastructure failure: the runner could not reach the ACR endpoint to authenticate, likely due to a momentary registry outage or runner network misconfiguration.
reproduction_steps: N/A - This is a transient infrastructure failure not reproducible locally.
recommended_actions: Re-run only the failed Docker job; the network issue is transient and typically resolves on retry.
If the failure recurs, check the status of openvinogithubactions.azurecr.io and the runner network configuration.
prevention_strategies: Add retry logic to the docker buildx build step to handle transient ACR authentication failures. Monitor ACR availability and runner network health.
historical_context: No prior occurrences found in the investigation database.
